negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing end-to-end heartbeat revival/deadline invariant: payload building does not hydrate/use HeartbeatTracker state, does not run a direct checkAndRevive phase, and does not gate remaining build phases immediately after that system transaction.
related_but_insufficient_code: HeartbeatTracker can hydrate from storage, and payload building can synthesize heartbeat revival transactions from parent storage.
Those revival transactions are enqueued as scheduled candidates, not executed as a direct pre-phase with a post-checkAndRevive deadline boundary.

rationale: The heartbeat candidates together define when the builder may rely on tracker state, when it must execute the heartbeat system transaction, and how the global deadline gates subsequent work.
statement: Before payload building or canonical scheduling uses HeartbeatTracker state, an unsynced tracker must hydrate from AgentHeartbeat contract storage; during payload building, configured heartbeat state with expired agents, or unsynced state with registered agents in storage, must trigger a direct checkAndRevive system transaction, and if the global deadline is exceeded after that transaction the remaining build phases must be skipped for the attempt.
suggested_falsification_tests: Unsynced tracker plus registered heartbeat storage should hydrate before payload use.
Expired tracker state should trigger a direct checkAndRevive system transaction.
Force deadline expiry after revival and assert later build phases are skipped.
summary: Heartbeat revival exists only as synthesized scheduled candidates; hydrated tracker and deadline-bound direct revival are absent.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Payload build with unsynced/expired heartbeat state; current port lacks tracker hydration and direct post-revival deadline gating.
title: Heartbeat revival uses hydrated state and respects build deadline
